ref: Jesus knew what they were up to, and he said, “Why are you trying to test me? Show me a coin!”
text: Yesu gi dɛsɛ yɛɛ mɛ kerɛ ansi daa ne mɛ kya taasɛ mɔ. Imɔso ɔ taasɛ mɔmɔ yɛɛ, “Menɛ so ne fɛ kya soo me idoo gɛnen? Ɔko ɔꞌ lɛɛ aterenbi gibi ba de nꞌ kerɛ.”
translated: When Jesus came down from the mountain, large crowds followed him. Then a man with a skin disease came and bowed before Jesus. He said, “Lord, you can heal me if you want.” Jesus reached out his hand and touched the man.

ref: Then they are no longer two people, but one. And no one should separate a couple God has joined together.”
text: Imɔso ɔnyen mɔ‑rɛ mɔ-ka mɛŋ baa mɛ gyɛ asa anyɔ, mɛ bingiri nyamesɛ kolon. Ibono Wurubuaarɛ ne n kyu mɔmɔ dabɔlɛ wɔra nyamesɛ kolon faa mɔ, ɔko ɔŋ sa ɔꞌ baragɛ mɔmɔ dɔ.”
translated: If you had not gone and brought the gun, the fetish would not have caught you. It is the Wurubuaare fetish that has caught you, and it will not let you go until you have died.

ref: Then the devil came to him and said, “If you are God's Son, tell these stones to turn into bread.”
text: Nfono mɔ, ilaa nyɛnyɛn gɛmu mɔ, mɔ ɔbono ɔ kya soo anyamesɛ idoo mɔ, gi ba mɔ asɛ baa tɔgɛ mɔ yɛɛ, “Nengyene gɛsintin fo gyɛ Wurubuaarɛ mɔ-bi mɔ, tɔgɛ abui adɛ yɛɛ aꞌ bingiri agyudɔ.”
translated: My son, in this very hour, the Lord has revealed to me that you will be a great leader, and he said to me, "I have chosen you to lead my people, so that you may bring them out of bondage."

ref: Simon and the others started looking for him.
text: Simon mɔ‑rɛ mɔ-nanboana mɛ naa ma‑a laarɛ Yesu.
translated: Simon and his companions went to look for Jesus.

ref: He answered, “Don't you know what I am talking about by now? You surely know that the food you put into your mouth cannot make you unclean.
text: Yesu gi lɛɛ gɛnɔ taasɛ mɔmɔ yɛɛ, “Imɔso fɛye kee fɛye-nwɔnsa dɔ iŋ ti bugi de fɛꞌ nu ilaa gɛsɛ? Imɔso fɛ mɛŋ nyi yɛɛ sɛi mɛŋ bo no i laa loo nyamesɛ gɛnɔ dɔ de iꞌ kyɔlesɛ mɔ-Wurubuaarɛ ɔson?
translated: Then Jesus said to them, “Why are you so afraid? Do you still have no faith? How is it that you have no faith and do not trust in the power of my Father, who is in heaven?”

ref: Everyone will hate you because of me. But if you keep on being faithful right to the end, you will be saved. The Horrible Thing (Matthew
text: Me so mɔ, ɔkamaasɛ laa kyo fɛye. Ɔbono ɔ laa yelɛ mɔ-ayaa so ka gyan me so kpaa lii mɔ, Wurubuaarɛ laa mɔlɛgɛ mɔ de ɔꞌ nyɛ ɔkyenaten mɔ asɛ. Nkɛ Nbono Dɔ-Lɛɛ Awɔrɔfɔɔ Mɔ A Laa Don Awɔrɔfɔɔ Kamaasɛ
translated: I am the chief, the one who holds the authority. The god that is in the river came and told me that Wurubuaare should give him a sheep. And the River God's Messenger and the Town Crier's Messenger are both present.

ref: As Jesus was leaving, he saw a tax collector named Matthew sitting at the place for paying taxes. Jesus said to him, “Follow me.” Matthew got up and went with him.
text: Yesu gi lii nfono ɔ kya kpe mɔ, o wu ɔnyen ɔko tɛ mɔ-gɛsun ɔwɔraten. Mɛ kya terɛ mɔ yɛɛ Matiyu. Gɛnen Matiyu mɔ gyɛ daa lɛnpoo sɔɔbo. Yesu gi wu mɔ gɛnen mɔ, ɔ tɔgɛ sa mɔ yɛɛ, “Baa buu me de foꞌ wɔra me-ɔkasɛbo.” Gɛsintin mɔ, Matiyu gi koso buu mɔ.
translated: When Jesus was passing by, he saw a tax collector sitting in his office. His name was Matthew. Jesus said to Matthew, "Follow me and be my disciple." Immediately, Matthew got up and followed him.

ref: So he told them, “Every student of the Scriptures who becomes a disciple in the kingdom of heaven is like someone who brings out new and old treasures from the storeroom.” The People of Nazareth Turn against Jesus (Mark
text: Yesu gi tɔgɛ sa mɔmɔ yɛɛ, “Nbara ɔŋmarasɛbo kamaasɛ nyi Wurubuaarɛ agyɛbi dedaa ne o kii nyɛ kasɛ Wurubuaarɛ gɛwuragyi dɔ akyenabi ilaa bɔla mɔ, ɔ dɛ fɛɛ gɛten wura ɔbono ɔ bo ilaa dedaa de ilaa pobɔrɔ pɛwu mɔ-ilaa ɔyelaten mɔ. Nengyene i gyɛ ilaa dedaa ilaa mɔ, ɔ bo nno, ne i kii i gyɛ ilaa pobɔrɔ ilaa kee mɔ, ɔ bo nno.” Yesu Gɛwi Awura Mɛ Mɛŋ Sɔɔ Mɔ Gyi
translated: And Jesus said to them, "When you pray, say: Our Father in heaven, hallowed be your name. Your kingdom come. Your will be done, on earth as it is in heaven. Give us each day our daily bread, and forgive us our sins, for we ourselves forgive everyone who is indebted to us. And lead us not into temptation." Jesus Christ Our Lord's Prayer

ref: You are a bunch of evil snakes, so how can you say anything good? Your words show what is in your hearts.
text: Fɛ dɛ fɛɛ awɔɔ abono a bo gibɔrɔ mɔ gikpen. Fɛye ilaa nyɛnyɛn awura adɛ, ilaa dɛnsɛ imɔ ne nan lii fɛye-nnɔ dɔ? Ilaa ibono i bo nyamesɛ gisen dɔ mɔ, imɔ ne ɔ kya lɛɛ tɔgɛ.
translated: If you see a toad running in broad daylight, know that something is after its life. So if you see your enemy's child, will you not save him? If you save him, God will bless you and your household will never lack.

ref: or visit you while you were sick or in jail?”
text: Gɛmenkɛ ne a wu fo fo kya lɔ abɛɛ mɛ tii fo de obu, ne a kpaa laa fo?’
translated: If we leave you here and go to the farm, and you die, who will bury you?

ref: Yes, Father, this is what pleased you.
text: Gɛsintin, me-sɛ. Imɔ ibono ne n wɔra fo ɔbolɛ.”
translated: Good morning, my friend. I am going to the farm today.

ref: After Jesus strictly warned the man, he sent him on his way.
text: Yesu gi kyɛ ɔnyen mɔ mɔ, ɔ tɔgɛ mɔ yɛɛ, “Naa fo kya kpe.” Ne o kii ka mɔ yɛɛ,
translated: When Jesus saw him lying there and learned that he had been in that condition for a long time, he asked him, “Do you want to get well?”

ref: When Jesus saw that a crowd was gathering fast, he spoke sternly to the evil spirit that had kept the boy from speaking or hearing. He said, “I order you to come out of the boy! Don't ever bother him again.”
text: Yesu gi wu yɛɛ sakpii mɔ kya nyaakyɔ ɔ kya too mɔ, ɔ tɔgɛ sa ilaa nyɛnyɛn oduduu mɔ yɛɛ, “Fo ilaa nyɛnyɛn oduduu ɔbono fo tɛ gɛbii gɛdɛ so, ne fo yɛgɛ gɛ bingiri gɛmugamuga de osintiribu mɔ, n kya tɔgɛ mi‑i sa fo yɛɛ, koso gɛmɔ so, n kerɛ fo faa, de foŋꞌ baa kii ba gɛmɔ so gɛkaako.”
translated: Before Jesus was taken up to heaven, he gave instructions to the men he had chosen as apostles by the power of the Holy Spirit. He said to them, "Do not leave Jerusalem but wait for the gift my Father promised, which you have heard me speak about. For John baptized with water, but in a few days you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit."

ref: When the woman got back home, she found her child lying on the bed. The demon had gone. Jesus Heals a Man Who Was Deaf and Could Hardly Talk
text: Gɛsintin mɔ, ɔkyii mɔ gi kii kpe gɛwi mɔ, ɔ kpaa wu mɔ-bi mɔ dɛ gifengen so; ilaa nyɛnyɛn oduduu ibono nkana i tɛ gɛbii mɔ so mɔ i koso. Yesu Gi Kyɛ Omuumuu
translated: But he was pierced for our transgressions, he was crushed for our iniquities; the punishment that brought us peace was on him, and by his wounds we are healed. Isaiah 53:5

ref: Now hurry! Tell his disciples he has been raised to life and is on his way to Galilee. Go there, and you will see him. This is what I came to tell you.”
text: Fɛꞌ kii belen kpaa tɔgɛ sa mɔ-akasɛbo mɔ yɛɛ o kyingi lii ibuni dɔ. Fɛꞌ tɔgɛ sa mɔmɔ yɛɛ ɔ laa gyangbara mɔmɔ kpe Galeli. Nno ne mɛ laa kii wu mɔ. Mɔ-ɔkalan ɔbono ɔ sa me yɛɛ nꞌ tɔgɛ fɛye mɔ ne.”
translated: I am going to tell you about the death of my grandfather. My grandfather died and was buried in Galeli. We all loved him. His children and family are the ones who told me this story.

ref: The girl left and asked her mother, “What do you think I should ask for?” Her mother answered, “The head of John the Baptist!”
text: Obolonbu mɔ gi lii kpaa taasɛ mɔ-nyi yɛɛ, “Menɛ ne nꞌ tɔgɛ wura mɔ yɛɛ ɔꞌ sa me?” Ne mɔ-nyi yɛɛ, “Tɔgɛ mɔ yɛɛ Gyɔn ɔbono ɔ gyere asa Wurubuaarɛ sagyere mɔ gimu ne fo kya laarɛ.”
translated: The elders asked him, "Who is this man who has done this to you?" He said, "The man who did this to me is John, the son of Zebedee, who is standing there among the disciples."

ref: Then the king will say to those on his left, “Get away from me! You are under God's curse. Go into the everlasting fire prepared for the devil and his angels!
text: “Nfono mɔ, nan tɔgɛ sa abono mɛ yelɛ me-benɛ gibaa so mɔ yɛɛ, ‘Fɛye berɛ, Wurubuaarɛ gi tɔgɛ nnɔ wɔra fɛye. Fɛꞌ naa me asɛ kpe ɔgya ɔbono ɔŋ kya wuꞌ gɛkaako mɔ dɔ! Wurubuaarɛ gi baala ɔgya ɔbono yela sa ilaa nyɛnyɛn pɛwu gɛmu mɔ mɔ‑rɛ mɔ-isɔɔ.
translated: My father, the matter that you have brought to me today, I have heard it, and I say, “Truly, the Lord God’s matter is truly a difficult one. But as for me, what can I do to help this matter? The Lord God Himself will help this matter to be completely resolved for us in His own time.”

ref: All of them saw him and were terrified. But at this same time he said, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
text: Mɔmɔ pɛwu mɛ wu mɔ mɔ, i ŋmaa mɔmɔ-asen. Ayaa abono so mɔ, Yesu gi tɔgɛ sa mɔmɔ yɛɛ, “Fɛꞌ keda nyoro. Me Yesu ne. Fɛŋ sa fɛꞌ selɛ gifuu.”
translated: Do not be afraid. I am your relative. The God above, Jesus by name, told me to tell you, “Be of good cheer. I am Jesus. Do not be afraid.”

ref: The Son of Man did not come to be a slave master, but a slave who will give his life to rescue many people. Jesus Heals Two Blind Men (Mark
text: I lii fɛɛ me, anyamesɛ pɛwu mɔmɔ-daa mɔ gbaa, meŋ ba gɛsinkpan so yɛɛ asa mɛꞌ son me. Nɛ ba daa de nꞌ baa son asa de nꞌ kyu me-nkpa kyɛɛ de iꞌ lɛɛ asa sakyɔ lii ilaa nyɛnyɛn giwɔra dɔ kiiri mɔmɔ sa Wurubuaarɛ.” Yesu Gi Bugi Agyaatanbo Anyɔ Ako Ansi
translated: I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me. If you really know me, you would know my Father as well. From now on, you do know him and have seen him." Jesus the Christ, the Son of the Living God.

ref: Jesus replied, “This very night before a rooster crows twice, you will say three times that you don't know me.”
text: Yesu gi tɔgɛ sa mɔ yɛɛ, “Sɔɔ gibui yela, ndɛ gɛnyɛ gɛdɛ pɛi de ikyaasɛ iꞌ baa folɛ ginyɔ mɔ, fo gbaa ne nan sorogɛ gikpadɔ gisaꞌ yɛɛ fo mɛŋ nyi me.”
translated: Then Jesus told him, “Go and do the same in your life.”

ref: He had often been put in chains and leg irons, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one could control him.
text: Gikpadɔ gifonɛɛ gifonɛɛ mɔ, asa mɛ da mɔ abaa de ayaa ilaran de ipan. Fɛɛ mɛ da mɔ ilaran de ipan gɛnen mɔ, ɔ kya wolaa gbaa-gbaa imɔ. Ɔ bo ɔlon gikyɔ ibono ɔko mɛŋ kya taalɛ mɔ.
translated: When the elephant breaks a branch and throws it down, the bush deer thinks it is meant for him. When the deer also breaks a branch and throws it down, it falls heavily. That is why the deer's back is full of marks.

ref: and said, “This man claimed he could tear down God's temple and build it again in three days.”
text: tɔgɛ yɛɛ, “Ɔnyen baarɛ gi tɔgɛ yɛɛ ɔ laa taalɛ boori Wurubuaarɛ ɔson obu mɔ de oꞌ kyu nkɛ nsa kii kyu yii mɔ.”
translated: Then he said, “The reason I am here is that I have come to ask Wurubua to give me a charm so that I can get money.”

ref: If a soldier forces you to carry his pack one kilometer, carry it two kilometers.
text: Ɔko gi nyise fo yɛɛ foꞌ sola mɔ-gɛsola sa mɔ gikpaaten kolon mɔ, sola sa mɔ de fo‑rɛ mɔ fɛꞌ naa akpaaten anyɔ.
translated: If you do not listen to the word of God, you will not be saved, and you will not enter the kingdom of God.

ref: But we don't want to cause trouble. So go cast a line into the lake and pull out the first fish you hook. Open its mouth, and you will find a coin. Use it to pay your taxes and mine.”
text: Imɔ‑rɛ imɔ gɛnen mɔ, aŋ kya laarɛ aꞌ wɔra too-too asɔɔbo mɔ ginyadon. Imɔso kpe ɔbon mɔ dɔ kpaa too daaruwa. Ɔken ɔbono fo laa daa gilɛɛ mɔ, kerɛ mɔ-gɛnɔ dɔ, fo laa wu aterenbi gibi. Lɛɛ gimɔ de foꞌ kyu kpaa too me‑rɛ fo-lɛɛ.”
translated: If you want to eat a toad, you must look for a fat and beautiful one. And when you have killed it, you must not put it on the ground. Because if you put it on the ground, it will become soiled and you will not enjoy eating it. So you must hold it in your hand until you have finished eating it.

ref: Jesus had gone into the temple and was teaching when the chief priests and the leaders of the people came up to him. They asked, “What right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
text: Imɔ gɛmara mɔ, Yesu gi kii kpe Wurubuaarɛ ɔson obu mɔ gikpaara mɔ so ɔ kya nyiile asa ilaa, ne Gyuda awura asunbi alɛɛbo abelɛnsɛ mɔ de mɔmɔ abelɛnsɛ sɛnsɛ ako mɛ ba mɔ asɛ baa taasɛ mɔ yɛɛ, “Ilaa ibonoana fo kya wɔra faa mɔ, anɛ ginyen dɔ ne fo kya wɔra imɔ daa? Anɛ ne n sa fo ɔkpa yɛɛ foꞌ wɔra imɔ?”
translated: When Jesus had finished saying all these things, he said to his disciples, “As you know, the Passover is two days away—and the Son of Man will be handed over to be crucified.” Then the chief priests and the elders of the people assembled in the palace of the high priest, whose name was Caiaphas, and they schemed to arrest Jesus secretly and kill him. “But not during the festival,” they said, “or there may be a riot among the people.”
